steps.title: Interpret the percentage increases for each species
steps.content:
Let the starting count of each species be $x$. When a quantity increases by 2,000%, the new value equals the original plus 2,000% of the original:
$$\text{Prey end} = x + 2000\% \cdot x = x + 20x = 21x$$
Similarly, a 250% increase gives:
$$\text{Predators end} = x + 250\% \cdot x = x + 2.5x = 3.5x$$

steps.title: Use the percentage comparison formula
steps.content:
When one quantity is $p\%$ greater than another, their ratio is:
$$\frac{\text{Larger}}{\text{Smaller}} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$
Here, prey end is $p\%$ greater than predators end:
$$\frac{21x}{3.5x} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$
Cancel $x$ and simplify:
$$\frac{21}{3.5} = 6 = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
Subtract 1 from both sides:
$$6 - 1 = \frac{p}{100}$$
$$5 = \frac{p}{100}$$
Multiply both sides by 100:
$$p = 500$$
